rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text:
Catching COVID can be an unpleasant experience for most people who catch it and can be deadly or leave people with long term conditions that are life limiting.  The cost both to to the country and the individual of COVID can be extremely high compared to the low cost of vaccination. Even if the vaccine does not stop infection it can reduce the severity.

Herd immunity is conferred on those who cannot be vaccinated and it is selfish to expose others to avoidable risks for no benefit to yourself.  There are no significant risks behind vaccines  and conspiracy theories are damaging national and global health.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text: I can see why such is policy is something that people would be interested in. Mandating covid vaccines could save many lives, ease the burden on the NHS and social care having to support people with long covid. However, such a policy would be totally unprecedented - no other vaccine is mandatory, and it would be hard to justify why just the covid one should be. Such a policy wouldn't pass a vote in either houses of government, and would be against public opinion. Even if it did pass it would probably be repealed by the next government, defeating the point of the policy. Finally, there are more constructive nudges the government could make, such as requiring it for travel abroad.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text: I'm divided on this issue. On the one hand, it makes sense to mandate vaccinations for all eligible citizens but on the other hand, I'm not sure that this is the best approach from the government. Certainly, it would make our country safer but we would still be at risk from others who are carrying the virus. I imagine there would be less chance of a major outbreak and having to quarantine and lock down again. I don't think it should be a government mandated scheme as people need to be able to make their own choices and not forced to do something that might be against their will.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text: We saw so much delay during Covid and who knows how many lives were lost due to indecision, conspiracies and so on. I remember a fitness instructor who didn't want the vaccine because he had believed some false information about the vaccine and he subsequently died. He almost certainly would have lived if he had had the vaccine. There is a responsibility on ourselves to act in the best interests of the whole public, and we saw too many people acting selfishly. Therefore let's take away the indecision and move more quickly by making it mandatory to be vaccinated. It would save lives.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text: I am pro vaccines but with the Covid vaccine I do feel that individuals should be able to make an informed decision, weighing up the benefits of receiving it.  I think the threat of Covid is still very present and it does make people very unwell, but I am not convinced that mandating vaccines is necessarily ethical for it.  The vast majority of people recover, and this is a huge cost to the government.  I think people also do have concerns regarding how quickly it was developed (whether or not it was created using existing vaccine methods), and some people have become unwell from having it.  It would be a difficult initiative to enforce and would probably lead to tensions.

rows.proposition: The UK should ban internet use for under-14-year-olds.
rows.text:
I feel that a ban on internet for kids under the age of 14 will have fundamentally positive impacts on their childhood with very little downside. Firstly, without social media, kids will be forced to use other avenues to socialise with their peers, reducing their reliance on instagram, and other social media platforms that allow them to skirt away from face to face interactions. In the digital age, and the rise in social media usage by the younger generations, has started having an impact on their social skills when face to face, and this is most likley in part due to their over reliance to use text based, emoji based communications rather than speak face to face or even via voice calls. reducing text based messaging and 'tiktok/reel' based communication could have a positive impact on their ability to express themselves and carry themselves in social situations. 

Secondly, removing socialmedia will prevent addiction cycles of doom scrolling and endless dopamine releases from just watching reels continuously. Which will be good for their brains and development. 
Lastly, no social internet access will prevent them from having exposure to harmful and radicalising websites and influencers, for exaple 4 chan, certain reddit threads, twitter and the sort. Or even bad infleuncing youtubers/streamers. This could allow the kids to grow up without a significant amount of influence which we now have today.

Instead, it's better to teach kids how to use the internet safeky and responsibly. Many children under 14 can use the internet well and they can hvae guidance from parents at home, or teachers at school. with the right support and controls i believe kids can get the benefits of the internet without too many risks. so i believee teaching and supervising is better than just banning.

rows.proposition: The UK should ban internet use for under-14-year-olds.
rows.text: Banning children from internet usage could help to protect them from dangers that are present online, like cyberbullying and predators making their online usage safer. It could also encourage the children to spend more time outside playing and connecting face to face with other children, which can be important in developing social and emotional developement. But such a ban could also limit theiraccess to useful resources, information and learning opportunities, and the ability to gain useful knowledge o digital skils which they may need in the future. Keeping them safe is one thing completely restricting the internet can hamper their development and learning opportunites.

rows.proposition: The UK should ban internet use for under-14-year-olds.
rows.text: It is quite a blanket and firm statement to make, regarding the banning of access to the Internet, for under 14 year-olds. The Internet is an important resource and medium for people of all age groups, and nobody should be limited from accessing it. Though there should absolutely be some pressure placed on parents to monitor, control and restrict Internet use if needs be. The burden ultimately, should be placed on the parents and not the government. While Government should offer strong and clear advice to parents, it is up to the parent to act and protect their children, while they use the Internet.

rows.proposition: The UK should ban internet use for under-14-year-olds.

rows.proposition: The UK should ban internet use for under-14-year-olds.
rows.text: I strongly disagree with the banning of internet use for children under 14.  The internet is an essential part of modern education and development, offering access to learning tools. creative platforms and opportunities for communication.  While online risks are real banning access completely would hinder digital literacy and isolate children from a key part of today's world.  A more balanced approach involves educating children about online safety, encouraging parental supervision, and using content filters to create age appropriate experiences.  Rather than implementing a blanket ban, we should aim to teach responsible and safe internet use from an early age to prepare children for the digital world they're growing up in.

Additionally, there would be more freedom for United Kingdom citizens and employees to travel and work across the European Union. Whilst there will be various hurdles to overcome to enable the UK to rejoin, and a high amount of legal processes involved, the overall benefit to the country outweighs the downsides

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: I am torn on this statement since I can see the health benefits of such a tax increase - both in combatting obesity, diabetes, dental problems and other issues. My instinct is to say that we should educate our way out of the problem rather than just increase taxes but - frankly - that has been tried and had little effect so it may be that a substantial price increase might help. One problem with this approach is that poor people will find their budgets even more stretched while rich people will be able to absorb a price increase without a problem. It will also hit hard those people who like to make their own wine or jam. I would prefer to see action taken on the sugar that is put in so many foodstuffs - often savoury ones - which are 'hidden' - people often consume a lot of sugar that way and their tastes are conditioned to expect foods to be sweeter than they are

There are high energy drinks that contain very large amounts of sugar or other sweeteners and maybe this should be something that is looked at first rather than just putting a tax on whatever drinks they want.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.

Additionally, healthier food options are often more expensive than their lower nutritional alternatives, and I feel that's something which needs to be addressed. Furthermore, I feel there could be a growth in the black market for such products. I can see that the intention is well-meaning, but I'm not sure it will work in practical terms.

rows.proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
rows.text: I disagree that the UK should reintroduce the death penalty. There is always a risk that the wrong person is convicted, and that an innocent person may be sentenced to death for no reason. In countries that have the death penalty (such as the USA), people sentenced to death may be on death row waiting for the punishment for years, which can be an expensive and costly process. Finally, the fact that death is an irreversible punishment needs to be considered - there can be no reversal ad brining back to life. If there were new evidence found etc, then nothing could be changed.

rows.proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
rows.text: I think it depends on the situation and the crime that has been committed; the death penalty is not relevant for all crimes. For instance, I think paedophilia or any child abuse cases should be punishable by death - especially where there is a chance of the offender recommitting such a crime. There are cases, such as murder cases, where the lines may be greyer. For example, if someone murders an innocent person in cold blood, it would be morally right to punish them by death. On the other hand, if someone murders someone who has wronged them, e.g. their rapist, it could be considered morally wrong to punish them by death.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly reduce immigration levels.
rows.text: Immigrants may select the UK as a destination for variable reasons. Those must be evaluated before passing any decision with regards to immigration. The UK is in need for skilled professionals in some fields, in which case, immigration is a solution to fill those gaps. Equally immigration might be to escape from financial and social situations or war. In such cases, refuge should be granted, as people do not have other choices. If the decision within the UK was to significantly reduce immigration levels, there may be repercussions related to the absence of skilled immigrants. All the same, the country can take measures to regulate immigration.

It is important not to rush this development so no stages are missed. An example is to allow young children to believe in Father Christmas, even although you know later they will have to understand he is a fairly story. Done properly, they will be able to hold two conflicting ideas in the head without bothering them. In this example, Father Christmas does not actual exist, but it is fun to go along with the idea during the Christmas period.

You want you team to win this season. They have never won before, but it is OK to still cheer them on in the hope they will will. world view.  2. You have a bad feeling about a situation, place or person you have just met. You have no facts to back up that feeling, however, it is wise to acknowledge that feeling and perhaps remove yourself from that situation while you seek clarification.
    As a child moves through these stages, they can often feel inadequate, different from everyone else; especially if they have negative words spoken to them. In trying to reconcile these negative feelings, they often look around for a explanation of how they are different from others around them. It can be easy to decide you are LGBTQ+ to explain these feeling and be drawn into that life style where others will accept you, even though if you had not had those negative feelings towards yourself you would not have. This is not to say that this is the reason all people decide they are LGBTQ+ but some do and then get very confused later as their feeling about themselves and attractions change.
    It is important when teaching about LGBTQ+ or gender and sex in general, that it is not taught as just a mechanical or biological thing with no emotional consequences.  While It is good to explain that it is not wrong to be curious and have feelings, however, experimentation without a full understanding can be dangerous. Examples could be experimenting with different sexual acts (especially when a younger person is coerced by an older person into doing something) or drugs that they are offered.

rows.proposition: The UK should formally apologize and provide reparations for the impacts of colonisation.

Unlike the monarchy of the past who would usually put British interests first the current monarchy put international interests first and neglect British interests and especially the peoples interests. They could improve this by honouring British culture and notable individuals. On an economical level I feel that they are a net negative as even though they do bring tourism to the UK, tourism would continue on regardless with or without them.
Currently the tax spent on providing them with a lavish life could be spent on more important things.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: I partly agree with this statement as the monarchy has been in place for many many years and abolishing it could lead to the UK climate changing drastically. There are a lot of tourists that come to the UK to see the royal family which  bolsters the economy due to tourism. However, most of the general opinion want the monarchy abolished because they are funded by the public just to upkeep an image. There are a lot of scandals that happen within the royal family so citizens may feel short-changed when they find that their money is funding a lifestyle whilst a majority of the country is living in poverty.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: I think its good to have a royal family for many reasons but the main one being that they don't change whereas the PM and ruling government does. The Queen was amazing as was revered the world over, and the Royals in general represent us on the world stage. Britain is known for being British and tradition is a big part of that, which is where the royal fmaily play a big part too with all the ceremony involved. As well as their representation they own and contribute to many charities accross the world and ultimately are a force of good.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: I think that a lot of the votes in this country currently are made by the elderly generation which is not helpful to society today. A lot of them are stagnating out economy, a lot of them are sat on assets or large sums of money,  bringing in triple locked pensions, and not actually spending a lot of that money in this country. They are only voting for things that benefit *ONLY* them, triple locked pensions wont benefit anyone else in this country. Younger people wont be blessed with that when they reach retirement age. Younger people are actually working and spending and contributing to the economy. In order for the country to thrive economically we need people out and spending, not sat at home with money in the bank. Younger people have more of an idea of what would help the economy since they are out there everyday. Older people tend to only vote for what they know. We would probably find that a different party would win the election and maybe some smaller parties would have a chance of even getting a coalition.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: In essence, it is a person's own choice whether to vote or not but we all have the privilege of voting so we should take that privilege and use it. It's very easy to not make the journey to the local polling station because you can't be bothered but there are some people in the world who could dream of being able to vote the way UK residents can. Choosing not to vote means that a prime minister will be elected not based on the wishes of the UK. We need as many people voting rather than 80% of the population; every vote counts.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text:
Elections are supposed to represent the will and beliefs of a nation in regard to how a country should be run. however, we often see that this is not the case and in the clearest examples, we see that an increase in voter turnout has repeatedly shown to change elections, showing some smaller elections are dubiously representative. 

voter disenfranchisement is an underhanded tool often used in politics to prevent another party's ability to acquire votes in certain areas, this may be due to a lack of access to voting booths or not having time to vote. mandatory, easy-access voting would prevent this as everyone would be required to show up and either vote or spoil their ballot.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text:
I think it should be made mandatory for several reasons:  

Some would be voters are too apathetic to vote but they should have a say as everyone should have representation regardless of their political leanings.  We would get voters from a wide range of demographics if it were compulsory.  There is no excuse as can send in a postal vote so whoever you are can vote assuming you are eligible.  It should mean a fair representation and it really would be the people's vote as currently the system is very flawed and shocking how few people actually vote, yet still moan if the party they ideally would lie do not get in!

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: The unions were once a powerful group, but they have become toothless in recent years. I put that down to corruption and backhanders to the egoists at the top. They tend to stand up for woke causes now and not British workers rights. They were formed to protect the worker but tend to attach themselves to the wrong causes, a bit like the modern Labour Party who aren't really Labour at all.  I don't think it would make any difference if they are strengthend or not. They are what they are. Their best days are behind them and that is very sad.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: Unions can represent and fight for the employee’s rights more effectively, so in this respect they are vital for having a checked balance between rights and obligations from both sides -employees and employers.   Labour unions also can advocate for different social justice causes .  However, having too much powers can be a way to cause more disruptions like strikes that can not only disrupt companies but the economy in general.  It is very common to see how a very powerful union can stop changes in the conditions or in the market that will have an impact in the ability to compete in a world that needs constant changes to be competitive in the market.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: I do not fully understand the current situation with labour unions in the UK, nor do I anticipate exactly what the implications of strengthening the labour unions with more rights and powers. I cannot think that it would be a bad thing to do this though. More rights and powers for employees in general could have a positive impact on the workplace. On the other hand, giving more power and rights to labour unions could complicate the structure of many companies, particularly the smaller ones. This could potentially lead to loss of profits due to fines and employee strikes which would not be good.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: Being a part of a trade union means that the worker, new or old, has access to knowledge of what the current situation of the trade is and can contribute to valuable and relevant discussions. Moreover, whenever policies related to the trade are announced, trade unions evaluate those and find what the impact of those policies might be. Then, they can voice their opinions and demand what would be in their favour. This means that a worker's rights are preserved by this representative entity that is separate from the government, employers, and policy makers. As a result, decisions are discussed and improved through suggestions and demands from trade unions and the rights of the workers are preserved.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: The U.K should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions in order to ensure that the health and safety standards of workplaces are regulated through enforcements of codes such as proper PPE. Furthermore, labour unions keep companies in line when it comes to paying minimum wage and not setting unlawful hours for workers. Not only this but also allowing adequate sick leave and holidays throughout the year. Additionally, with limited powers labour unions are not able to enforce rights for workers when it comes to discrimination either within the workplace or upon recruitment or redundancy. Overall it is clear that labour unions are vital to the workforce and strengthening their rights and powers would serve to aid in the safety and security for workers and their families.

I mean, the powers that be had the UK waving the Ukraine flag on and off social media when most people are not even aware where Ukraine is in the first place. Or the history of this war. The Ukraine leader - if he's still around, I don't keep up with news so I am not sure -  seems highly suspicious in character to me too. This is not our war to win and for goodness’s sake we have enough trouble with issues within our country to deal with. Let's do that before we offer help else where.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
rows.text:
I do not think the Ukraine have done anything wrong.  Russia want to take land which is not theirs even though Russia is a huge country.  Putin has waged a war that most people do not want including his own people.  it is causing a huge loss of life on both sides.  He is an oppressor of his own people and anyone who had stood up to the regime has been severely punished. 

I think supporting the Ukraines is the humane and moral thing to do as i think they are the type of country who would do the same thing and stand up for what is right.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
rows.text: I wholeheartedly support this statement. Ukraine suffered a completely unprovoked assault by Russia, and has been left single-handedly fighting a war against an aggressor with wider ambitions to take territory in Europe - rebuilding the Russian Empire. Ukraine doesn't have the resources to continue to fend off the Russian assault indefinitely and relies on support from other countries, and it's vital that the UK does all it can to help. We've already provided funding, but this will be for nothing if we don't provide the fullest possible bacing and Ukraine ultimately falls. If it were to fall, the offensive would likely progress across other parts of Europe and we'd be required to commit even greater resources in order to see off this increasing threat. It would be shameful if the heroic people of Ukraine were to fall to Russia and we hadn't done all that we could do as a country to support them.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
rows.text: UKRAINE'S SOVEREIGNTY IS UNDER DIRECT THREAT, AND SUPPORTING THEM HELPS DEFEND INTERNATIONAL LAWS THAT PROTECT INDEPENDENT NATIONS FROM BEING INVADED. IF COUNTRIES ALLOW SUCH AGGRESSION TO GO UNCHECKED, IT SENDS A DANGEROUS SIGNAL THAT BORDERS CAN BE VIOLATED WITHOUT CONSEQUENCES. BY PROVIDING SUPPORT, THE UK STRENGHTHENS ITS ALLIANCES, SHOWING COMMITMENT TO NATO AND GLOBAL PARTNERSHIPS, WHICH HELPS MAINTAIN COLLECTIVE SECURITY. ADDIOTIONALLY, ONGOING CONFLICT IN UKRAINE HARMS EUROPEAN STABILITY, AFFECTING ECONOMIES AND INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS. INCREASING MILITARY SUPPORT COULD HELP BRING THE WAR TO A QUICKER RESOLUTION, REDUCING SUFFERING AND LONG TERM INSTABILITY.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
rows.text: I think that this war is only going to end one way unfortunately, and while it's important we have taken in refugees and done our part, we also have to realise this war needs to end, and for it to end we need less fighting and more talking, i can't see a scenario where Russia admit defeat, and it's just a matter of time before they get the lend they want. we need a deal with Russia and can revisit it in the future when Putin is no longer around. Ukraine for their part need to make some compromises, War is bad, but this is how it goes throughout history, land is won by wars.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text: The BBC supports charities and raises money for causes such as children in need, which has a positive impact on many people's lives and so without funding they may not be able to continue doing this which would cause some people to suffer. The BBC also has a website dedicated to education and learning such as BBC bitesize, which many students rely on. They offer content from Key stage 1 right up to high school meaning they offer a large range of content. This can be useful for children who are home schooled as well as others who wouldn't have access to other educational support should it not be for the BBC and so I think its important that their funding isn't cut.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text: I think that while the BBC has faults, it is overall something to protect. It is a British institution and has produced some of the finest drama and comedy entertainment that the UK is known for across the world. If the BBC did not exist it would further concentrate all media being owned by a small group of American companies. The BBC also produces documentaries and niche content that others do not because there would be no commercial interest to. I feel there is some issues with the BBC's news coverage and how it so strongly favours the establishment and government, and if this was addressed the BBC as an institution would once again be very popular in the UK.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text: The BBC has been infamous for the protection of very prolific criminals, whose acts were known by executives and brushes aside for the sake of making money. Also the spread of certain propaganda, one of which being the demonization of the working class population for other working class citizens to gawp, laugh or scold at. One example being the Miner's Strike during Margaret Thatcher's time as Prime Minister, in which miners who were trying to afford a decent wage in order to feed their families were made out to be hooligans who attacked the police as well as violent protestors.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text:
If the funding is appropriate then there is no reason to defund it as we do need media to understand what's going on. 
But if funding can be reduced and use it in other departments which is much mire needed like health, housing, education, etc then they should definitely take action. That said defunding and reducing funding are different things. There should be clear reason and evidence to support why they are considering or trying to defund or reduce funding which should be clearly stated and voted with public. As BBC has its name for the longest and oldest standing media and do have reputation and trust of people.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text: The issue of funding for the BBC is multifaceted and does not have a simple answer. In light of the recent proliferation of platforms, many of which produce extremely high quality programming, the question for many is why the BBC is still being funded by taxpayer money. On the other hand, the BBC has become an institution and is synonymous with British culture for many abroad and within Britain. Defunding the BBC would lead either to its demise or to it having to look for other revenue streams such as advertising, which would for many cheapen the quality of its output and be a detriment to British culture and an established British institution.

rows.proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
rows.text: BBC broadcast content that is loved by the people. It is not a profit driven media but produce content that serves the interest of people. It has high reputation and is recognized internationally. It provide content that are accurate reliable and in depth something that will be lost should UK decide to defund it. BBC provide equal access to information. They provide content that cuts across from educational politics documentaries and thus promoting culture and diversity. Their covering is wide and the commercial broadcasters cannot meet the requirement of their audience. It has a high number of audience and therefore defunding it will be unfair.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text:
A four day week could be a real asset to some people if they are able to work their hours into the 4 days instead of working 5,  If not then they may end up earning less money and the employer is willing to pay the same wage for less hours which would be at great cost to some companies. Some jobs may not afford the employer to work 4 days for example teachers have to work the 5 days of the week that the children attend school, if that was to change then it would cause massive problems for working parents.
Work productivity may increase for some people but some companies may not benefit from a break in productivity levels with a shorter working week or staff may become more stressed during the 4 days to make up for the loss of one working day.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: Workers on a 4 day week will have to produce the same outcome of a 5 day week with the time spent at work. Now this can be longer 4 days or it could be achieving the same outcomes for 5 days in 4 days. Hence the output stays the same. There may ne some savings by not having staff in for a day a week, but this will depend on the type of industry. This effectiveness in the 4 day week will make the company more agile and responsive. The benefits to the worker is the extra time off to address the work life balance, which in my opinion is better with a 4 day working week as the two days are not enough to reset the batteries and do all the chores needed for the following week.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I don't agree with this statement. From my perspective this statement would seem to imply that the UK government would mandate a 4 day week, which would be totally outside of what I think the remit for government involvement in the economy should be in a liberal country. I'm also not convinced that this would have any positive impact as it restricts the rights of those workers happy to work 5 or more days in a week. It would almost certainly have a negative economic impact as if there were any productivity or cost saving efficiencies to be made by such a program, I suspect the private sector would have already adopted such a scheme.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: Although I could be supportive of this because it would suit me, the organisation I work for has a mix of staff who work varying hours.  Half of our workforce does 9-5 and half do shifts over a 24 hour period.  I do not see how we could make it fair to all employees.  We are a charity and funded to deliver services which have very little room in them for us to make a surplus so it would add costs and potentially mean that we are losing money on the services we run which would mean that we can no longer deliver on them.  This would leave many people in our communities without access to mental health support and put further stress on the NHS.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text:
I think many people can feel demotivated working full time over 5 days. Work can become monotonous and staff can lack   motivation.  By having 3 days break over a week would allow people time to focus on family, hobbies and their home. I consider that a job should not be your life! Working 4 days would also save many people on commuting costs/childcare.
Working the same job over a condensed 4 days would necessitate better time management,  working more effectively to get the job done.  I know many jobs, including ones I have had in the past, that a lot of time is wasted on non work and clock watching. 4 day weeks would not only benefit employees but employers too.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Humankind has developed greatly from their beginnings as a basic organism in the oceans. We have gone from a time when there was little respect for human beings and animals lower down the (social) scale. People have more empathy and sympathy to their fellow man/woman than ever before and creating this could only encourage more good men/women to provide a more egalitarian, hopeful, caring and positive society. Individuals with greater wealth would have slightly less power and more people would be encouraged to better themselves from a higher base. The UK should lead the world in compassionate, dynamic values that are the heart of a myriad, cosmopolitan society.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Idealists seem to believe that with a universal basic income for all then yes some people would want more and work to generate money, but that most people would be happier and would still contribute to society in the best way they could. I do not believe most would. Many people would do little for society as now, but would also not attend their current jobs which help society function. I do not believe that the system would work, at least in a large society such as the UK. There would not be enough income generated overall for the government to then pay a basic income to all that would be anywhere near at a liveable level.  The country is already running out of money in providing a minimum income guarantee for pensioners, plus benefits to all who claim to be sick or need help. Basically nice idea, maybe once we have World peace and harmony (so never).

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: There are several countries from my understanding that have implemented UBI and the results were positive. I believe that if UBI was to be implemented, there would have to be caution exercised, with the main focus being getting people into jobs. In theory, if people had the money to sufficiently support themselves, healthcare systems would ideally struggle less, as there will be a healthier population. In the same vein, children would live more enriching, safer and healthier lives as the parents would not have to find money. Allowing people the time and freedom to search for a job as well, without worrying about money, would in turn boost the economy. It could ultimately be seen as an investment in the future.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Only for certain income earnings below certain amount as these group of people will have difficulty with paying bills, working long hours, child care, distance.  For students because firstly when they go to university the work load is mentally and physically draining, living costs, travelling cost, loan for the course, wellbeing cost.  Once they start work, its difficult as the starting income is low but cost of living is high eg. bills, rent, food, travel.  With doing long hours at work they do not have time to look at hobbies because of costs.  It is very stressful for young adults.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
The state the UK is in right now is morally reprehensible, there are thousands and thousands of children who go hungry every day while there are still millionaires and billionaires who make more money than a reasonable person could ever spend in their lives. 

UBI has been shown by many countries to be an effective remedy to unemployment, poverty, and healthcare. so many issues span from people being poor and therefore, becoming a burden on a nation's finances. these issues could be prevented if every person received a basic security payment to ensure they can afford food, heating and medicine.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
rows.text: I believe there is a case for compulsary covid vaccination as with past deseases such as measles which was almost wiped out until scare stories about mmr reduced the uptake and it made a comeback . Our N.H.S. is already over loaded especially in the winter months when covid is more likely to emerge along with other seasonal illnesses . I also think that the more people who are protected the less the virus is likely to spread among large groups thereby reducing the need to close pubs and restaurants to help  curb the spread which would also not have such a huge impact on the economy .

rows.proposition: The UK should legalise euthanasia.
rows.text: I have a great deal of sympathy for those who want to see euthanasia legalised in the UK. Nobody want to see someone's last days and months to be painful ones.  And it seems only fair that those suffering be given the freedom to choose.  But this choice is a false one whilst we do not have high-quality end-of-life care for everyone. The choice is also a moot one if patients feel under pressure to end their life prematurely to avoid the financial burden of caring for them. Until palliative care is massively improved, I do not think euthanasia should be legalised.

rows.proposition: The UK should legalise euthanasia.
rows.text: I do agree in part that it should be legalised as it offers the right to a choice that is granted to everyone else if they wish to commit suicide in other ways, but in a way that is safer and more comfortable. It would allow people who are terminally ill or otherwise chronically suffering to end their pain with dignity and comfortably. However, I can also recognise that legalising this would raise several ethical issues and risk further complications. For example, it risks being a 'slippery slope' where people may feel coerced into euthanasia by others, or could even be used as a method to kill someone against their will with an 'excuse'.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: I believe that with consent from guardians, some minors should be able to buy more violent video games as some children like such games and are more mature than others. Furthermore these games can have major benefits for their social life and they can make so many friends, from instance from school if they were to play games such as GTA 5. If the parents know and are o with it with them ministering their childs playtime and if they aren't acting and changing due to the games violent nature I see no few problems in such a thing. It would also be difficult to impose such regulations as minors will find a way to still play such games

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: I quite strongly disagree with the idea that the UK government should impose stricter regulations on violent video games to minors. To my knowledge, there is no significant scientific evidence proving that violent video games directly cause violent behaviour in children. The UK already has a pretty robust rating system though the PEGI rating scheme, which labels and age rating to aid the consumer and retailer. I think it would be much more effective to focus on parental guidance and education rather than enacting new laws. Parents are best placed to decide what's appropriate for their children as they know their kids values and how mature they are.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: Young people can be very impressionable and playing violent video games probably doesn't help with their development. If a younger person sees something violent in a video game, they may feel the need to react to it in some way and may want to reenact it in real life. However I also believe there should be an element of responsibility from the parents to ensure they are on top of what their child is playing and that they are ensuring their safety. There should be regulations and shops need to adhere to these, but the parent should definitely also take responsibility for this.

rows.proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
rows.text:
First of all, I'm against death penalty, human life is simply too valuable to just take it away. I would love to see prisoners being instead involved in some wider country improvement programs; US system - although flawed - involves prisoners in contributing to the country - be it by small scale manufacturing ar social work. UK is not utilising those opportunities.
On top of that there is always a doubt when it comes to the sentence, and although I believe most of sentences are correct, mistakes can happen - that is not defining factor against death penalty for me though.

rows.proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
rows.text: This is a tough one and I can see both sides to the argument. There are miscarriages of justice, such as the Lucy Letby case, she might have been hanged back in the day. But there are clear cases, such as the Southport killer, who I'd be happy to see hanged, electrocuted or poisoned. People like that will never change, have no remorse and are of no use to society. So why keep them in prison for 50 years or more? I therefore think it would be appropriate it some cases. Pointless discussion though - never gonna happen in the UK as liberal bed wetters have taken over.

rows.proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
rows.text: I think the death penalty is an easy way out and is a very extreme punishment. If someone has murdered, for example, I do believe they do not deserve to be here on this planet. However, I think they should suffer and rot in prison, supporting the aim of retribution. Also, innocent people could wrongly get accused and receive the death penalty as seen in cases in America. Therefore, if the death penalty were to be ever reintroduced solid factual evidence would have to be provided. On the other hand, the death penalty is a good deterrence to have in society, and I would expect offending and re-offending would reduce as a result.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly reduce immigration levels.
rows.text: whilst legal immigration can bring lots of benefits to the country, in respect of filling skilled job vacancies and making the country more diverse. Surely given the number of people unemployed we should be ensuring that children are well educated and unemployed people trained to take on vacancies. We are a small country and it feels increasingly difficult to access health care and for people who were born here to get housing. Lots of the traditions and customs of our country have been diluted - such as companies avoiding saying Easter or Christmas etc. As for illegal immigration we have to get tough, far too many single men who have different understanding and beliefs in respect of women are allowed to stay. The cost to the country is too much and any person who arrives illegally should never be given the full benefits of a person born here or a legal immigrant

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly reduce immigration levels.
rows.text: Immigration levels are a very difficult and emotive topic.  On the one hand I can see arguments to limit especially with the resulting burden on both the benefit and health systems on the country.   However, immigrants also provide vital contributions to the workforce and help create a more diverse society allowing different views.  Further with a decreasing birth rate and ageing population do we have enough people in the country to fill jobs that we need people to do in the country.  Overall perhaps there should be a targeted approach to limit immigration to those individuals who can add to the economic envelopment of the country.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly reduce immigration levels.
rows.text: The country is in obvious decline. A controlled demolition of the British way of life. One of the reasons for pointless wars and regime change operations seems to be to generate migration. This migration is profited on and the leads to division and chaos. People no longer trust their neighbours and our cities become no go zones. Hard working people are expected to pay for declining services and home ownership becomes unaffordable. The new arrivals who work reduce wages and put pressure on services and housing. This is an experiment that has failed. People are labelled as bad people for telling the truth

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum.
rows.text: Including feminist perspectives in the National literature curriculum would be a very positive thing to do, it would help to broaden students understanding of literature and also challenge the traditional male narratives. It would promote critical thinking which can only be positive, having the views of feminists along side the traditional male narrative would promote critical thinking about gender and power. The inclusion of feminist perspectives would have to be implemented thoughtfully, we would have to make sure that we ensure that diverse feminist voices and perspectives are represented in order to make sure that the approach is inclusive of all students. Feminist writers have had an important role in our society so it is only right that the national literature curriculum shows that.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum.
rows.text: A study of literature should seek to embrace all genres of which there are good quality examples/ feminist literature includes some excellent writing, for example Jane Austen Hilary Mantel, Zadie smith l and Kate Atkinson. No student of literature would be complete without the inclusion of feminist perspectives, which has traditionally  been underepresented at best and totally ignored at worse in my many curricula. You men in particular would grow up knowing much more and understanding much more about feminism and, indeed, being a woman if they studied literature from that perspective. Early studies of feminist literature could be very revealing and quite exciting

rows.proposition: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school.
rows.text: Children have a right to an inocent childhood without being force fed information regarding heterosexual or homosexual life styles. They are unlikely to full understand either at a young age and may form opinions which  later lead to confusion. They should be allowed to come to their own conclusion over time snfd then whatever their choice they should by all means be given help and guidance as to how to follow these up and live a happy life content in their own skin and free from outside influence and prejudice. Only that way will they feel fullfilled and very content

rows.proposition: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school.
rows.text: It should really be down to parents to decide if their children learn about contentious subjects like this. There will be a lot of parents that won't want their children to learn about this, as they may see it as encouraging children to consider being transgender, when it would be something they would not have considered otherwise. Some parents will disagree that a person can change gender and will see this as brainwashing their children at an age where they are accepting of anything they're told. However, learning more about this at a young age, could reduce bullying in later years, if children know more about this and as we celebrate equality, this could be seen to be part of that and essential. However, I do think overall that parents should get to voice their opinions on strong subjects like this and it would likely be too divisive to implement this at primary age.

rows.proposition: The UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity.
rows.text: Although I am strongly in favour of sex-based rights for women, and want to see separate spaces based on sex in certain contexts for example hospitals, prisons, rape crisis centres, I still believe that transgender people have rights too. By being recognised and protected in law (which I believe transgender identities already are in the UK), this does not have to have a negative impact on women who wish to uphold single sex spaces. It should be possible to find a solution whereby there are spaces for all identities. As a marginalised and somewhat persecuted group, I would not want transgender people to feel like they aren't being recognised for who they are as this can be devastating.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text:
The spending increase enables the UK to have a stronger say and input with our NATO counterparts. It will send a clear message to others that the UK is ready and able to defend itself should the need ever arise.
Increased spending will also unite us with our NATO allies showing them that the UK is fully committed to the alliance.
The increase in spending will allow the UK to invest in the latest technologies and advanced weaponry needed in this day and age to keep up with the ever changing evolution of weaponry which would be needed if we ever had to go to war. We also need the extra money to train all NATO nations to have a united front and to work together seamlessly should we be required to do so.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text: There are currently wars, such as in Ukraine, Iran, and Israel, which could leave the UK very vulnerable and a target for foreign attacks. Therefore it is important that the UK should strengthen its international relationships, solidify its role within NATO, and protect itself against potential attacks, in particular nuclear bombing. However, the downside to this is that the UK may alienate countries outside of NATO and give the impression that they are militarily preparing to defend itself against attack. While ethically I don't really agree with bolstering the UK military, I can see that it is an unfortunate necessity of our times.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: While the UK monarchy is usually characterised as being largely ceremonial and symbolic, they still hold a considerable amount of power and influence. King Charles III, when he was Prince of Wales, is known to have quietly influenced government decisions. In a true democracy, there should not be any person whose power comes solely from their lineage, as even with the best will in the world they can never truly represent the people of that nation. Add to that the monarchy holds huge amounts of wealth and land (the crown estates covers something 200,000 acres), and makes money from it all. People who argue that the monarchy costs the country very little, and even is a profit because of tourism, fail to recognise that we can keep the palaces and royal parks even if the monarchy itself is removed.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: The monarchy continues to become outdated the farther we head into the century, especially when considering how much money from the taxpayer is used for lavish celebrations for coronations and weddings or renovating the palace. They may draw in tourism, but they are still a family who, to me, do not earn that kind of power — a power that seems quite underused to begin with when it comes to laws, etc — unlike democratically-elected leaders such as a Prime Minister or President. However, it is not as simple as one would hope, as the assets would more than likely going to the family rather than the state.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: There is no good reason to compel people to vote in elections, and in a free society , we should be free to not vote of we wish.  Many people have no interest in politics, or don't support any party, so it would be pointless demanding that they vote.  Practically this would be impossible to arrange.  Many people would be physically unable to vote, by reason of illness, or absence.  This is an unnecessarily draconian measure, which would have no benefit to the democratic process, and would only serve to alienate the ordinary person.  What would the penalties be for non-compliance?

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.

This could be because, compared to other countries, the United Kingdom is actually a good place to live, whoever is in power.  It would take a pretty bad series of events to compel people to vote voluntarily, and if they won't vote of their own accord, the chance of getting them to vote with any seriousness if they were forced to is slight.  In the current age of social media, if people were forced to vote it would be far too easy to sway the population to vote for an idiotic ideal.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: I believe the UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions, and I would rate my agreement of 90 out of 100. Stronger unions are essential for protecting workers rights, ensuring fair pay, safe working conditions, and reasonable working hours, especially in sectors where employees are more vulnerable to exploitation. By balancing between employees, unions help prevent abuses and promote more equitable treatment across the workforce. They also contribute to economic justice and come equality , and supporting collective bargaining that uplifts low and high earners voice in key decisions that affect their lively hood and well being.

rows.proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
rows.text: Perhaps it is the press coverage they receive or perhaps I read the wrong newspapers but the monies paid to some of these union leaders and the perks they receive are totally disproportionate to their abilities to negotiate "reasonable"  deals or to accept in may cases what appear to be more than reasonable pay and condition settlements. There is quite often a disconnect between ordinary union members and union leaders. The closed shop would appear to still be in place in certain areas and the present voting systems perhaps should be looked at. The days of "Red Robbo" may have gone but there is still an element of intimidation and coercion present. On the other side of the coin employers are quite often not blameless.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: Legislating for a four day working week for all makes very little sense. Most commercial work is demand driven or cyclical, for this reason the inflexible approach of a four day week would not work. Another major problem with such an approach is pay, because unless people are going to receive the same amount of money for a four day week as they would have done for a five day week, and therefore work the same number of hours in longer days, people will be worse off and may not be able to pay basics such as a mortgage or rent. The labour market definitely needs reforming, however this approach is not the way forward.

I think it would boost family life because people would see their children more and get to spend more time with them. So all these reasons make me think it could be a good idea.

The world is changing and more and more costs are in every country's sights. The amount would have to be carefully worked out, but should ensure the rent can be paid and household expenses met.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: The concept of UBI is similar to the concept of communism.  Everybody treated in the same way.  Except that it never works that way in communist countries and UBI would not create equality here either.  The rich would get the money but it would be irrelevant, the poor would get it but it wouldn't be enough to make their lives good.  Therefore it is hard to understand why UBI would be seen to be a good thing.  It is just benefits as they are now but dressed up in wording to make it sound like something more.  UBI experiments carried out around the world have failed to prove that it is a good, workable idea
